seoTitle: Point Frederick vs Point Piper — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Point Frederick and Point Piper. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Point Frederick vs Point Piper: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Point Frederick and Point Piper.
heading: Point Frederick vs Point Piper — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Point Frederick, New South Wales and Point Piper, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Point Frederick has a lower overall climate risk score (44/100) compared to Point Piper (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Point Frederick or Point Piper safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Point Frederick has a flood risk score of 38/100, while Point Piper scores 54/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Point Frederick scores 45/100 for bushfire risk. Point Piper scores 26/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Point Frederick or Point Piper?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Point Frederick has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.15% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
